description: v1.2.0 perf baseline (P3-T15). Captured against the stress-test-10k fixture (10000 ConfigMaps + 2000 Deployments + 2000 owner-ref ReplicaSets + 400 Services) across Tier 1 (in-memory) and Tier 2 (PostgreSQL + Apache AGE), on Docker Desktop Kubernetes under WSL2. Tier 2 numbers go through kubectl port-forward + WSL2 networking (~10ms/round-trip vs native Linux); Tier 1 is benched against a local memory-backed process. spec_targets are reclaimed to the v1.0 README-promised latencies now that store-side pushdown aggregation has removed the per-request whole-graph materialisation that forced the Phase 2 relaxation. Caveat: the Tier 2 stress-test-10k namespace_view p95 (~1.4s) exceeds the namespace SLO because the synthetic fixture puts all ~10K resources in a single namespace — the documented 100%-single-namespace artefact where the NamespaceSubgraph JOIN costs more than a full scan. Real namespaces are far smaller, and cluster_view (the realistic whole-graph aggregation) lands at 360ms p95; Tier 1 is unaffected.

spec_targets._comment: v1.2 reclaims the latency targets to the figures the v1.0 README publicly promised. Phase 2 relaxed them to 5000-8000ms while cluster_view / namespace_view still materialised the whole graph per request; the store-side pushdown aggregation removed that cost, so the targets return to cluster_view / namespace_view p95 <= 1000ms and blast_radius p95 <= 500ms. This is the final instalment of the debt the pushdown work took on.
